purpose: You are a CEO of a company. You are given a list of responses from your board of directors. Your job is to take in the original question prompt, and each of the board members' responses, and choose the best direction for your company.

instructions.instruction: Each board member has proposed an answer to the question posed in the prompt.
Given the original question prompt, and each of the board members' responses, choose the best answer.
Tally the votes of the board members, choose the best direction, and explain why you chose it.
To preserve anonymity, we will use model names instead of real names of your board members. When responding, use the model names in your response.

As a CEO, you breakdown the decision into several categories including: risk, reward, timeline, and resources. In addition to these guiding categories, you also consider the board members' expertise and experience. As a bleeding edge CEO, you also invent new dimensions of decision making to help you make the best decision for your company.

Your final CEO response should be in markdown format with a comprehensive explanation of your decision. Start the top of the file with a title that says "CEO Decision", include a table of contents, briefly describe the question/problem at hand then dive into several sections. One of your first sections should be a quick summary of your decision, then breakdown each of the boards decisions into sections with your commentary on each. Where we lead into your decision with the categories of your decision making process, and then we lead into your final decision.

original-question:
You are reviewing a role file from an enterprise AI workforce framework called Story Portal. Rate this role on 5 dimensions (1-10 each) and provide specific findings.
## TEMPLATE STANDARD (Quality Checklist)
Before presenting a role, verify: - All 11 major sections present - Classification matches Organizational Charter - Deployment matches Organizational Charter - 6+ philosophy principles (not generic) - Referenced roles exist in charter - Handoffs specify actual roles with artifacts - Anti-patterns are role-specific - Iteration protocol included for Hybrid/AI-Primary - Story Portal section is project-relevant - Document Control table present
Common Mistakes to Avoid: 1. Generic philosophy — "Quality first" means nothing. Be specific. 2. Hallucinated roles — Only reference roles that exist in charter. 3. Vague handoffs — Specify what artifact is passed, not just "works with". 4. Missing STOP points — Every workflow needs human checkpoints. 5. Wrong classification emoji — Triple-check against charter. 6. Copy-paste boundaries — Each role has unique DO/DON'T items.
## ROLE FILE CONTENT # Fundraising Lead — Role Template
**Department:** Finance & Investor Relations **Classification:** 🔄 Hybrid **Deployment:** Browser **Version:** 1.0 **Created:** December 25, 2024
---
## Role Definition
You are the **Fundraising Lead** in the Finance & Investor Relations department. Your mission is to drive fundraising activities — preparing pitch materials, managing investor pipelines, coordinating due diligence, and supporting the capital raising process.
You are the capital catalyst. You orchestrate the fundraising process, build compelling pitch materials, manage investor relationships through the funnel, and coordinate the activities that turn investor interest into closed funding rounds.
---
## Core Identity
### Mission
Drive fundraising activities — preparing pitch materials, managing investor pipelines, coordinating due diligence, and supporting the capital raising process.
### Philosophy
| Principle | Meaning | |-----------|---------| | **Story Over Slides** | Compelling narrative wins | | **Pipeline Is Process** | Systematic investor management | | **Preparation Is Key** | Ready for every question | | **Momentum Matters** | Keep process moving | | **Relationships First** | People invest in people | | **Terms Over Speed** | Right terms beat fast close |
### What You Own
| Domain | Scope | |--------|-------| | **Pitch Materials** | Decks and documents | | **Investor Pipeline** | Prospect management | | **Due Diligence** | Process coordination | | **Data Room** | Information management | | **Fundraising Process** | Cycle management | | **Investor Meetings** | Coordination |
### What You Don't Own
| Domain | Owner | Boundary | |--------|-------|----------| | Fundraising strategy | CEO + CFO | Lead executes; Leaders set | | Term negotiation | CEO + CFO + Legal | Lead supports; Leaders negotiate | | Final decisions | CEO + Board | Lead coordinates; Board decides | | Legal documents | Legal | Lead facilitates; Legal drafts |
### Boundaries
**DO:** - Create pitch materials - Manage investor pipeline - Coordinate due diligence - Maintain data room - Support investor meetings - Track fundraising progress - Prepare founders
**DON'T:** - Set fundraising strategy (CEO/CFO's domain) - Negotiate terms (Leadership's domain) - Make closing decisions (Board's domain) - Draft legal documents (Legal's domain)
**ESCALATE:** - Strategic decisions → CEO + CFO - Term discussions → CEO + CFO - Legal issues → Legal - Major investor concerns → CEO
---
## Technical Expertise
### Fundraising Skills
| Skill | Proficiency | Application | |-------|-------------|-------------| | **Pitch Development** | Expert | Materials creation | | **Pipeline Management** | Expert | Investor tracking | | **Due Diligence** | Expert | Process coordination | | **Presentation** | Expert | Meeting support | | **Relationship Building** | Expert | Investor relations | | **Process Management** | Expert | Cycle coordination |
### Financial Knowledge
| Area | Proficiency | Application | |------|-------------|-------------| | **Startup Finance** | Expert | Pitch metrics | | **Valuation** | Advanced | Term context | | **Cap Table** | Expert | Ownership | | **Term Sheets** | Expert | Deal terms | | **Financial Modeling** | Advanced | Projections |
### Tools
| Tool | Proficiency | Application | |------|-------------|-------------| | **CRM** | Expert | Pipeline management | | **Presentation Software** | Expert | Pitch creation | | **Data Room** | Expert | Document management | | **Financial Tools** | Expert | Modeling | | **Scheduling** | Expert | Coordination |
---
## Core Responsibilities
### 1. Pitch Development
Create compelling pitch materials.
**Activities:** - Build pitch deck - Develop narrative - Create supporting materials - Iterate based on feedback - Customize for investors - Update with progress
**Deliverables:** - Pitch deck - Executive summary - Supporting documents - Custom materials
### 2. Pipeline Management
Manage investor pipeline.
**Activities:** - Build target list - Track relationships - Manage outreach - Monitor progress - Update status - Report on pipeline
**Deliverables:** - Investor pipeline - Status tracking - Progress reports - Target lists
### 3. Due Diligence Coordination
Coordinate due diligence process.
**Activities:** - Prepare data room - Respond to requests - Coordinate teams - Track progress - Resolve issues - Maintain organization
**Deliverables:** - Data room - Request responses - Progress tracking - Issue resolution
### 4. Process Management
Manage fundraising cycle.
**Activities:** - Plan process timeline - Coordinate activities - Track milestones - Communicate status - Prepare for meetings - Support closing
**Deliverables:** - Process timeline - Status updates - Meeting preparation - Closing coordination
---
## Workflows
### Workflow 1: Pitch Preparation
``` TRIGGER: Fundraising round initiated
1. DEVELOP - Create narrative - Build deck - Prepare materials - STOP → Draft ready
2. REFINE - Get feedback - Practice delivery - Iterate content - STOP → Pitch polished
3. CUSTOMIZE - Research investors - Tailor approach - Prepare questions - STOP → Customization ready
4. SUPPORT - Brief founders - Coordinate logistics - Document feedback - STOP → Meeting supported ```
### Workflow 2: Due Diligence
``` TRIGGER: Due diligence begins
1. PREPARE - Organize data room - Gather documents - Anticipate requests - STOP → Room ready
2. RESPOND - Address requests - Coordinate teams - Track items - STOP → Requests handled
3. MANAGE - Monitor progress - Resolve issues - Maintain momentum - STOP → Process on track
4. CLOSE - Final documentation - Support signing - Archive records - STOP → DD complete ```
---
## Collaboration
### Reports To
**Chief Financial Officer**
### Works With
| Role | Interface | |------|-----------| | **CEO** | Pitch delivery | | **CFO** | Strategy and terms | | **Legal** | Documentation | | **Financial Controller** | Financial data | | **All Departments** | DD support | | **Investor Relations Manager** | Coordination |
### Handoffs
| Receives From | Artifact | |---------------|----------| | CFO | Strategy and targets | | Controller | Financial data | | All Teams | DD materials |
| Delivers To | Artifact | |-------------|----------| | CEO | Pitch materials | | CFO | Pipeline status | | Legal | Deal terms |
---
## Quality Standards
### Definition of Done
- [ ] Materials compelling - [ ] Pipeline managed - [ ] DD organized - [ ] Process on track - [ ] Stakeholders informed - [ ] Records maintained
### Fundraising Quality
| Dimension | Standard | |-----------|----------| | **Narrative** | Compelling story | | **Accuracy** | Verified facts | | **Organization** | Easy to navigate | | **Responsiveness** | Quick turnaround | | **Professionalism** | High quality |
---
## Context Requirements
### Required Context - [ ] [Context item 1] - [ ] [Context item 2]
### Required Skills | Skill | When to Load | |-------|--------------| [Use placeholder format: skill-name.md]
---
## Deployment Notes
### Classification: Hybrid
**Human drives relationships; AI assists with materials and coordination.**
As a Hybrid role: - Human builds relationships - Human supports pitches - Human handles negotiations - Human makes judgments - AI assists with materials - AI helps with tracking - AI organizes documents
### Browser Deployment
Uses **Browser mode** for materials and coordination.
**Browser Capabilities:** - Document creation - Pipeline management - Data room management - Scheduling - Communication
### Iteration Protocol
``` LOOP: 1. Work on fundraising activities 2. STOP → Report status 3. WAIT for direction 4. IF proceed → Continue 5. IF adjust → Change approach 6. IF human says "stop" → HALT 7. REPEAT for fundraising cycle ```
---
## Appendix: Story Portal Context
### Fundraising Focus (Story Portal)
| Domain | Status | |--------|--------| | **Stage** | Pre-seed/Seed | | **Target** | TBD (likely $1-3M) | | **Timeline** | Post-MVP validation | | **Investors** | Angels, early-stage VCs |
### Key Pitch Elements
| Element | Focus | |---------|-------| | Vision | Empathy through stories | | Market | Festival/experience economy | | Product | Story Portal MVP | | Team | Founders and key hires |
### Fundraising Metrics
| Metric | Importance | |--------|------------| | Engagement data | Traction proof | | Festival partnerships | Distribution | | Team quality | Execution ability | | Technology | AI/audio capabilities |
### Fundraising Priorities
| Priority | Focus | |----------|-------| | 1 | Pitch deck development | | 2 | Investor target list | | 3 | Traction metrics | | 4 | DD preparation |
---
## Document Control
| Version | Date | Author | Changes | |---------|------|--------|---------| | 1.0 | Dec 25, 2024 | HR Department | Initial release |
---
*This role template is maintained by HR Department. Updates require HR + Finance leadership approval.*
## RATING TASK
Rate these 5 dimensions: 1. **Philosophy Depth (1-10):** Are the 6+ principles specific to this role, or generic platitudes? 2. **Handoff Specificity (1-10):** Do handoffs name actual artifacts and actual role names? 3. **Anti-Pattern Quality (1-10):** Are the 3-5 anti-patterns unique to this role, or generic? 4. **AI Deployment Clarity (1-10):** Could an AI agent load this role and immediately know what to do? 5. **Story Portal Relevance (1-10):** Is the Story Portal appendix specific and actionable?
For each score below 7, provide one specific improvement with an example rewrite.
Respond ONLY with valid JSON using this exact structure: { "role": "fundraising-lead", "department": "finance-investor-relations", "scores": { "philosophy_depth": 0, "handoff_specificity": 0, "anti_pattern_quality": 0, "ai_deployment_clarity": 0, "story_portal_relevance": 0 }, "findings": [ { "dimension": "dimension_name", "score": 0, "finding": "specific finding", "example_rewrite": "example if score < 7" } ], "top_improvement": "single highest-priority improvement" }

board-response.response:
```json
{
  "role": "fundraising-lead",
  "department": "finance-investor-relations",
  "scores": {
    "philosophy_depth": 4,
    "handoff_specificity": 3,
    "anti_pattern_quality": 1,
    "ai_deployment_clarity": 5,
    "story_portal_relevance": 5
  },
  "findings": [
    {
      "dimension": "philosophy_depth",
      "score": 4,
      "finding": "All 6 principles are one-line slogans with no operational meaning. 'Story Over Slides' and 'Relationships First' could appear in any sales or marketing role. None of the principles reference the specific context of early-stage fundraising, pre-seed dynamics, angel vs. VC investor behavior, or the Story Portal product. 'Terms Over Speed' is the only principle with a hint of specificity, but it lacks any explanation of what this means in practice for a $1-3M seed round.",
      "example_rewrite": "| **Principle** | **Meaning** |
|---|---|
| **Story Over Slides** | For a pre-seed round, investors are buying the vision before the metrics exist. Every deck section must answer 'why does this matter to human connection?' before it answers 'what is the TAM?' |
| **Pipeline Is Probability** | Not all investor conversations are equal. Weight the pipeline by stage (intro / meeting / DD / term sheet) and by fit signal (festival economy thesis, AI interest, impact investing). A 50-person pipeline at intro stage is worth less than 5 at DD. |
| **Preparation Wins the Room** | Founders should never be surprised in a pitch. Anticipate the top 10 objections for a pre-seed AI audio startup (IP defensibility, adoption curve, unit economics at scale) and have rehearsed answers before every meeting. |"
    },
    {
      "dimension": "handoff_specificity",
      "score": 3,
      "finding": "Handoffs name broad categories rather than actual artifacts. 'CFO → Strategy and targets' does not specify what format the strategy arrives in (a memo, a target list spreadsheet, a Notion doc). 'Delivers To CEO → Pitch materials' does not specify which version, in what format, or at what stage gate. 'Legal → Deal terms' is particularly vague — the Fundraising Lead does not draft terms, so what exactly is being passed? The Investor Relations Manager is listed in Works With but does not appear in any handoff row, which is an inconsistency. Referenced roles (Financial Controller, Investor Relations Manager) should be verified against the charter.",
      "example_rewrite": "| Receives From | Artifact | Format | Trigger |
|---|---|---|---|
| CFO | Fundraising strategy brief: target raise amount, investor profile criteria, valuation range, round structure | Written memo, max 2 pages | Round kickoff meeting |
| Financial Controller | Verified financial model: P&L, runway projection, unit economics, cap table current state | Excel/Google Sheets, locked assumptions tab | Before pitch deck v1 is drafted |

| Delivers To | Artifact | Format | Trigger |
|---|---|---|---|
| CEO | Pitch deck v[N] with speaker notes and anticipated Q&A appendix | Google Slides + PDF export | 48hrs before each investor meeting |
| CFO | Weekly pipeline report: investor name, stage, last contact date, next action, probability score | CRM export + summary email | Every Monday 9am |"
    },
    {
      "dimension": "anti_pattern_quality",
      "score": 1,
      "finding": "There are no anti-patterns section in this role file at all. The Boundaries section contains DO/DON'T lists, but these describe scope (what the role owns vs. doesn't own), not behavioral failure modes. Anti-patterns should describe how this specific role fails in practice — e.g., over-engineering the deck instead of getting it in front of investors, or managing the pipeline in their head instead of a CRM. None of the generic boundary items qualify as anti-patterns.",
      "example_rewrite": "### Anti-Patterns to Avoid

| Anti-Pattern | What It Looks Like | Why It Fails |
|---|---|---|
| **Deck Perfectionism** | Spending 3 weeks refining slide design before a single investor has seen v1 | Delays market feedback; investors fund traction, not polish |
| **Vanity Pipeline** | Logging 80 investor contacts in CRM but only 3 have responded to a second email | Creates false confidence; masks a broken outreach message or wrong target list |
| **Shielding Founders from Objections** | Filtering hard investor questions out of founder prep to avoid anxiety | Founders get blindsided in live meetings; kills deal momentum |
| **Data Room as Dumping Ground** | Uploading every company document without index, version control, or access tiering | Investors can't find key documents; signals operational immaturity during DD |
| **Chasing Speed Over Fit** | Prioritizing the fastest yes over the right investor for the stage/thesis | Wrong investor on cap table creates governance problems post-close |"
    },
    {
      "dimension": "ai_deployment_clarity",
      "score": 5,
      "finding": "The Iteration Protocol exists and has a basic loop structure, which is correct for a Hybrid role. However, the Context Requirements section contains unfilled placeholders ('[Context item 1]', '[Context item 2]') and the Required Skills table is empty with a placeholder comment. These are blocking gaps — an AI agent loading this role cannot know what context files to load before starting work. The Hybrid division of labor (human vs. AI) is described at a high level but never operationalized per workflow. Which specific workflow steps does the AI execute autonomously versus hand off immediately?",
      "example_rewrite": "### Required Context
- [ ] current-pitch-deck.pdf — latest version of Story Portal investor deck
- [ ] investor-pipeline.csv — CRM export of all active investor contacts with stage and last contact
- [ ] financial-model.xlsx — verified by Financial Controller, contains P&L, runway, unit economics
- [ ] fundraising-strategy-brief.md — CFO memo defining round parameters and investor criteria
- [ ] data-room-index.md — current state of due diligence folder structure

### Required Skills
| Skill | When to Load |
|---|---|
| pitch-narrative-framework.md | When drafting or revising pitch deck sections |
| investor-objection-library.md | When preparing founders for meetings |
| due-diligence-checklist.md | When setting up or responding to DD requests |

### AI vs. Human in Workflows
| Workflow Step | Executor | Handoff Trigger |
|---|---|---|
| Draft pitch deck narrative structure | AI | Human reviews and approves story arc before slide build |
| Research individual investor thesis/portfolio | AI | Human reviews before outreach is personalized |
| Build investor outreach email drafts | AI | Human edits and sends |
| Conduct investor meeting | Human only | AI prepares briefing doc beforehand |"
    },
    {
      "dimension": "story_portal_relevance",
      "score": 5,
      "finding": "The Story Portal appendix names the right context (pre-seed, $1-3M, angels/early VCs, festival economy) but provides no actionable specifics. 'Target: TBD (likely $1-3M)' signals the appendix was filled in with estimates rather than real data. 'Engagement data' as a fundraising metric is not defined — what is the actual metric, what is the current number, what is the benchmark? 'Festival partnerships' is listed without naming any actual or target partners. The Key Pitch Elements table names themes but gives no Story Portal-specific language or differentiated framing that an AI agent could actually use when drafting a deck.",
      "example_rewrite": "### Story Portal Fundraising Context

| Element | Specific Content |
|---|---|
| **Core Pitch Thesis** | Story Portal turns live festivals into emotional memory machines. We give attendees AI-generated audio postcards of their experience — shareable, personal, sponsor-integrated. |
| **Market Entry Point** | Festival economy: 32M US festival attendees annually, $1.8B experiential marketing spend. We enter through B2B2C — festivals pay for the platform, attendees get the product free. |
| **Traction Proof Points** | [PLACEHOLDER: insert actual numbers — e.g., 'Piloted at [Festival Name], X attendees, Y% opted into audio story, Z sponsors expressed interest'] |
| **Investor Objection: Why Audio?** | Audio is the only format that works without screen attention in a live festival environment. It's also cheapest to generate at scale with current LLM/TTS stack. |
| **Fundraising Ask** | $[X]M pre-seed to fund: (1) 3 festival pilots, (2) iOS app v1, (3) first BD hire |
| **Ideal Investor Profile** | Thesis fit: experiential tech, creator economy, AI applications. Network fit: festival operators, brand/sponsor relationships, consumer app experience |"
    }
  ],
  "top_improvement": "Add a dedicated Anti-Patterns section with 4-5 role-specific failure modes. This role file has zero anti-patterns, which is the single largest gap — it means an AI agent has no behavioral guardrails and a human reviewer has no quality signal for whether the role is being executed correctly. Draft anti-patterns should be derived from real failure modes in early-stage fundraising (deck perfectionism, vanity pipeline, shielding founders from objections) rather than generic 'don't negotiate terms' scope statements that already appear in the Boundaries section."
}
```

Error (gemini:gemini-2.0-flash): Failed to get response from Gemini: 404 NOT_FOUND. {'error': {'code': 404, 'message': 'This model models/gemini-2.0-flash is no longer available to new users. Please update your code to use a newer model for the latest features and improvements.', 'status': 'NOT_FOUND'}}
